basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Yūnus — a Meccan sūra of 109 verses — opens on the letters “Alif, Lām, Rā,” of which Qushayrī makes the keys of the divine Names: Alif for “Allāh,” Lām for “Laṭīf,” Rāʾ for “Raḥīm”. It is the wise Book, promised on the Day of the primordial Covenant. The sūra unfolds the cosmic signs — the sun-radiance and the moon-light, the alternation of night and day, the water sent down that makes the earth green and is then harvested — as so many calls to meditation. It confronts the denial of the Meeting and the forgetfulness that follows rescue, and offers the great parable: a ship caught in the tempest, where people call upon God in sincerity — only to forget Him as soon as they are saved. It announces the friends of God (awliyāʾ Allāh): “no fear shall be upon them, nor shall they grieve”; and celebrates the people of Jonah — the sole city whose faith was accepted at the eleventh hour, by the absolute primacy of mercy over work, for “by His mercy they reached their supplication, not by their supplication His mercy”. It relates Noah, Moses and Pharaoh — “today We save you in your body that you may be a sign for those who come after you.” It is sealed on the Qurʾān-admonition, healing for what is in the breasts, guidance and mercy — and on the prophetic command: to follow the revelation, to be patient, to entrust oneself to the best of judges.
themes: the keys of the divine Names
cosmic signs and the appointed term
the people of Jonah and mercy
the friends of God without fear or grief

versets.ar: الر ۚ تِلْكَ آيَاتُ الْكِتَابِ الْحَكِيمِ
versets.tr: alif lām rā tilka āyātu l-kitābi l-ḥakīmi
versets.trad: Alif. Lām. Rā. These are the verses of the wise Book.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Alif is the key of the Name “Allāh,” the Lām the key of the Name “Laṭīf” (the Subtle), the Rāʾ the key of the Name “Raḥīm” (the All-Merciful). God swears by these Names that this Book is the one promised to you on the Day of the primordial Covenant. The time of promises is fulfilled, He says: let the lovers hasten to the cup, and stand firm on the path of the Beloved.

mots.def: isolated letters, keys of the divine Names per Qushayrī.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ح ك م

versets.ar: أَكَانَ لِلنَّاسِ عَجَبًا أَنْ أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَىٰ رَجُلٍ مِّنْهُمْ أَنْ أَنذِرِ النَّاسَ وَبَشِّرِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَنَّ لَهُمْ قَدَمَ صِدْقٍ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ ۗ قَالَ الْكَافِرُونَ إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَسَاحِرٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: a-kāna li-l-nāsi ʿajaban an awḥaynā ilā rajulin minhum an andhiri l-nāsa wa-bashshiri lladhīna āmanū anna lahum qadama ṣidqin ʿinda rabbihim qāla l-kāfirūna inna hādhā la-sāḥirun mubīnun
versets.trad: Is it so astonishing to people that We inspired one of their own to warn his fellows and to announce to the believers that a true recompense awaits them with their Lord for their sincerity? The disbelievers say: “This man is a manifest sorcerer!”
versets.commentaire: Three things, says Qushayrī, astonished them: the possibility of resurrection after death, the sending of messengers to creatures, and the singling out of Muḥammad among all men. Had they known the perfection of His royalty, they would have denied none of this. As for the “foot of sincerity” (qadam ṣidq) with the Lord, it is, in one sense, what they have advanced for themselves in sincere acts of obedience; in another sense, what the True has set forward for them out of His eternal solicitude — for the feet of aspirants raised for God hold sanctity with Him.

mots.racine: ق د م / ص د ق
mots.def: true standing preceding the servant with his Lord.

versets.ar: إِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ اللَّهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ اسْتَوَىٰ عَلَى الْعَرْشِ ۖ يُدَبِّرُ الْأَمْرَ ۖ مَا مِن شَفِيعٍ إِلَّا مِن بَعْدِ إِذْنِهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمُ اللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ فَاعْبُدُوهُ ۚ أَفَلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: inna rabbakumu llāhu lladhī khalaqa l-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa fī sittati ayyāmin thumma stawā ʿalā l-ʿarshi yudabbiru l-amra mā min shafīʿin illā min baʿdi idhnihi dhālikumu llāhu rabbukum fa-ʿbudūhu a-fa-lā tadhakkarūna
versets.trad: Truly your Lord is God (Allāh), who created the heavens and the earth in six days, then established Himself upon the Throne, directing every affair. There is no intercessor save after He has given His permission. Such is God, your Lord — so worship Him! Will you not reflect?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: His act needs no duration — and how could it, when time itself is among His creatures? “Then He established Himself upon the Throne” means that He has singled Himself out by the majesty of Greatness and the attribute of Sovereignty — an image brought close for human understanding. He directs the affair: no associate supports Him, none repels what He has decreed. “Such is God, your Lord”: this is recognition; “so worship Him”: this is the charge — one through His teaching, the other through His granted success.

mots.racine: س و ي / ع ر ش
mots.sens: He established Himself upon the Throne

mots.racine: د ب ر / أ م ر
mots.def: all things proceed from His measure.

versets.ar: إِلَيْهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ جَمِيعًا ۖ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقًّا ۚ إِنَّهُ يَبْدَأُ الْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُ لِيَجْزِيَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ بِالْقِسْطِ ۚ وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَهُمْ شَرَابٌ مِّنْ حَمِيمٍ وَعَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْفُرُونَ
versets.tr: ilayhi marjiʿukum jamīʿan waʿda llāhi ḥaqqan innahu yabdaʾu l-khalqa thumma yuʿīduhu li-yajziya lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū l-ṣāliḥāti bi-l-qisṭi wa-lladhīna kafarū lahum sharābun min ḥamīmin wa-ʿadhābun alīmun bi-mā kānū yakfurūna
versets.trad: To Him you all return. Such is the promise of God in all Truth. It is He who originates creation, then renews it, that He may reward with equity those who have believed and worked righteous deeds. As for those who have refused to believe, a scalding drink and a painful chastisement await them as the price of their disbelief.
versets.commentaire: Return presupposes a beginning of souls before they were placed in bodies: they had, in the abodes of glorification and sanctification, a station, and one who returns from a journey to his homeland leaves a trace among his loved ones. Qushayrī: as for the obedient who returns to God, it is nearness, reward, and the Most Beautiful; as for the rebel who returns to his Lord, it is in the garb of bankruptcy — there he finds the robe of forgiveness, of veiling, and of safety, for his Master's mercy is better for him than all his asceticism.

mots.racine: ب د أ / ع و د
mots.sens: He originates creation, then renews it

mots.racine: ش ر ب / ح م م

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ الشَّمْسَ ضِيَاءً وَالْقَمَرَ نُورًا وَقَدَّرَهُ مَنَازِلَ لِتَعْلَمُوا عَدَدَ السِّنِينَ وَالْحِسَابَ ۚ مَا خَلَقَ اللَّهُ ذَٰلِكَ إِلَّا بِالْحَقِّ ۚ يُفَصِّلُ الْآيَاتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī jaʿala l-shamsa ḍiyāʾan wa-l-qamara nūran wa-qaddarahu manāzila li-taʿlamū ʿadada l-sinīna wa-l-ḥisāba mā khalaqa llāhu dhālika illā bi-l-ḥaqqi yufaṣṣilu l-āyāti li-qawmin yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: It is He who made the sun a radiance and the moon a light, and assigned its phases that you may know the number of years and the reckoning. God did not create that save in Truth. He sets forth the signs for a people who know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds this verse in inner symbols: the lights of the intellects are stars, projectiles against the demons; those of the sciences are moons, waxing and waning; those of the gnoses are suns whose rising illumines the secrets of the gnostics (ʿārifūn). And: the sun of day sets at night, but the sun of hearts never sets. The moon that waxes, reaches its plenitude, then fades, also figures the servant now in contraction and now in expansion, now in sobriety and now in effacement.

versets.ar: إِنَّ فِي اخْتِلَافِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ وَمَا خَلَقَ اللَّهُ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ لَآيَاتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: inna fī khtilāfi l-layli wa-l-nahāri wa-mā khalaqa llāhu fī l-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi la-āyātin li-qawmin yattaqūna
versets.trad: Truly, in the alternation of night and day, and in all God has created in the heavens and on the earth, are signs for a people who fear Him.
versets.commentaire: Day distinguished itself by its brightness, night by its darkness, without merit or fault on their part. Qushayrī reads herein the proof that refusal and acceptance, prevention and arrival, are conditioned by no cause, acquired by no act: they are pure Will and Decree. Day is when the heedless absorb themselves in their gains, and the Near ones withdraw to contemplate their Lord — “He is the Sun, only that every sun disappears, while the One we mean does not disappear”. Night, for the lover, is the hour of intimate confidence; for the rebel, of complaint.

mots.racine: خ ل ف / ل ي ل / ن ه ر

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ لَا يَرْجُونَ لِقَاءَنَا وَرَضُوا بِالْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَاطْمَأَنُّوا بِهَا وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ عَنْ آيَاتِنَا غَافِلُونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna lā yarjūna liqāʾanā wa-raḍū bi-l-ḥayāti l-dunyā wa-ṭmaʾannū bihā wa-lladhīna hum ʿan āyātinā ghāfilūna
versets.trad: Those who do not hope for Our meeting, who are content with the life of this world and find their ease therein, those who remain heedless of Our signs —
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they do not hope for the Meeting because they did not desire it; they did not desire it because they did not love; they did not love because they did not know; they did not know because they did not seek; and they did not seek because He willed that they not seek Him. Conversely: had He willed that they seek Him, they would have sought Him; had they sought, they would have known, loved, desired, and hoped for His Meeting. The people of this world were content with it and were deprived of the Garden; the ascetics clung to it and were deprived of Nearness.

mots.racine: ر ج و / ل ق ي
mots.sens: they do not hope for Our meeting
mots.def: refusal of the desire for God.
finding one's repose in this world.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ مَأْوَاهُمُ النَّارُ بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ
versets.tr: ulāʾika maʾwāhumu l-nāru bi-mā kānū yaksibūna
versets.trad: they shall have the Fire as their refuge for what they have earned.
versets.commentaire: The refuge of the deniers is the Fire — not by injustice but by what they themselves have earned. Separation (firqa) from God is already the hell of the heart; the Fire is but its outer manifestation.

mots.racine: أ و ي / ن و ر

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ يَهْدِيهِمْ رَبُّهُم بِإِيمَانِهِمْ ۖ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهِمُ الْأَنْهَارُ فِي جَنَّاتِ النَّعِيمِ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū l-ṣāliḥāti yahdīhim rabbuhum bi-īmānihim tajrī min taḥtihimu l-anhāru fī jannāti l-naʿīmi
versets.trad: But those who believe and work righteous deeds, their Lord guides them by their faith. Rivers shall flow at their feet in the Gardens of Bliss.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: as He has guided them today to His knowledge without any intermediary, He will guide them tomorrow to His Garden and His reward without any helper from the creatures. The obedient have their light running before them; the angels welcome them. And the True says to the contrite: “My servants, the people of the Garden today are too occupied by their reward, the people of the Fire too overwhelmed by their chastisement, to welcome you — but if none guides you, I will guide you. For if I dealt with you according to your merit, where would the generosity due to My right be?”

mots.racine: ه د ي / أ م ن
mots.sens: their Lord guides them by their faith
mots.def: faith is the instrument of guidance.

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ن ع م

versets.ar: دَعْوَاهُمْ فِيهَا سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ وَتَحِيَّتُهُمْ فِيهَا سَلَامٌ ۚ وَآخِرُ دَعْوَاهُمْ أَنِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: daʿwāhum fīhā subḥānaka llāhumma wa-taḥiyyatuhum fīhā salāmun wa-ākhiru daʿwāhum ani l-ḥamdu lillāhi rabbi l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: Their invocation there shall be: “Glory to You, O God!”, their greeting: “Peace!”, and the close of their invocation: “Praise be to God, Lord of the Worlds!”
versets.commentaire: Their speech is the praise of God — such is their state at the moment of the Meeting. And their greeting, in that state, comes from God: “Peace be upon you”. Their final invocation is praise, which here is eternal and perpetual praise. The True greets them with an eternal peace and an unending word — He, the Mighty, the Absolute, the Glorious, the One.

mots.def: greeting of the people of the Garden.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ يُعَجِّلُ اللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ الشَّرَّ اسْتِعْجَالَهُم بِالْخَيْرِ لَقُضِيَ إِلَيْهِمْ أَجَلُهُمْ ۖ فَنَذَرُ الَّذِينَ لَا يَرْجُونَ لِقَاءَنَا فِي طُغْيَانِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law yuʿajjilu llāhu li-l-nāsi l-sharra stiʿjālahum bi-l-khayri la-quḍiya ilayhim ajaluhum fa-nadharu lladhīna lā yarjūna liqāʾanā fī ṭughyānihim yaʿmahūna
versets.trad: If God hastened evil for people as they hasten after good, their term would already be fulfilled; but We leave those who do not hope for Our meeting to wander blindly in their rebellion.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: had We answered people when they curse themselves in anger or impatience, We would have hastened their ruin; but We take upon Ourselves not to answer them, and out of mercy toward them We do not hear their invocation. Often the servant complains that his Lord does not grant his wish — if he knew that this refusal is tenderness, and that the granting would have been affliction, he would fall silent. “People turned away from us without wrong or cause; they thought ill of us — why did they not think well?”

mots.racine: ط غ ي / ع م ه
mots.sens: in their rebellion they wander blindly

versets.ar: وَإِذَا مَسَّ الْإِنسَانَ الضُّرُّ دَعَانَا لِجَنبِهِ أَوْ قَاعِدًا أَوْ قَائِمًا فَلَمَّا كَشَفْنَا عَنْهُ ضُرَّهُ مَرَّ كَأَن لَّمْ يَدْعُنَا إِلَىٰ ضُرٍّ مَّسَّهُ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ زُيِّنَ لِلْمُسْرِفِينَ مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā massa l-insāna l-ḍurru daʿānā li-janbihi aw qāʿidan aw qāʾiman fa-lammā kashafnā ʿanhu ḍurrahu marra ka-an lam yadʿunā ilā ḍurrin massahu kadhālika zuyyina li-l-musrifīna mā kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: When affliction touches a human being, he calls upon Us on his side, sitting, or standing; but when We remove from him his affliction, he passes on as though he had never called upon Us about the harm that touched him. Thus are the deeds of the transgressors made fair-seeming in their eyes.
versets.commentaire: When trial tries the servant, the state tears him out and pushes him to seek deliverance; he then knows that none other than God will save him, and necessity wrenches from him a sincere recourse. But when God removes that for which he was crying, the very rest of deliverance distracts him from that burning state — he becomes as if he had never been in pain. Qushayrī concludes: a trial that throws you straight before your Beloved profits you more than a gift that makes you forget Him.

mots.racine: م س س / ض ر ر

mots.sens: as if he had not called upon Us

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَهْلَكْنَا الْقُرُونَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ لَمَّا ظَلَمُوا ۙ وَجَاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ وَمَا كَانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِي الْقَوْمَ الْمُجْرِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad ahlaknā l-qurūna min qablikum lammā ẓalamū wa-jāʾat-hum rusuluhum bi-l-bayyināti wa-mā kānū li-yuʾminū kadhālika najzī l-qawma l-mujrimīna
versets.trad: We destroyed before you generations when they acted unjustly. Their messengers had brought them clear proofs, but they would not believe. Thus do We requite the guilty folk.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “If injustice were a house in the Garden, God would send ruin upon it.” Injustice is placing a thing outside its place: when the servant, for his needs, directs his intention toward creatures and hangs his heart on them, he places the heart outside its proper place. The chastisement of this injustice is the ruin of the heart: the way of return to God closes — for if he returned to Him, He would suffice; yet he persists in clinging to creatures and remains weaned from God, without ceasing to need them: misery upon misery.

mots.racine: ه ل ك / ق ر ن

versets.ar: ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَاكُمْ خَلَائِفَ فِي الْأَرْضِ مِن بَعْدِهِمْ لِنَنظُرَ كَيْفَ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: thumma jaʿalnākum khalāʾifa fī l-arḍi min baʿdihim li-nanẓura kayfa taʿmalūna
versets.trad: Then We made you their successors on earth, that We might see how you would act.
versets.commentaire: We have informed you of the fate of those who came before you, that you may take heed and be saved; and whoever takes no heed will himself serve as a lesson to his successors. Every place of succession on earth is a veiled examination.

mots.racine: ن ظ ر / ع م ل
mots.sens: that We may see how you act

versets.ar: وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتُنَا بَيِّنَاتٍ ۙ قَالَ الَّذِينَ لَا يَرْجُونَ لِقَاءَنَا ائْتِ بِقُرْآنٍ غَيْرِ هَٰذَا أَوْ بَدِّلْهُ ۚ قُلْ مَا يَكُونُ لِي أَنْ أُبَدِّلَهُ مِن تِلْقَاءِ نَفْسِي ۖ إِنْ أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا مَا يُوحَىٰ إِلَيَّ ۖ إِنِّي أَخَافُ إِنْ عَصَيْتُ رَبِّي عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-idhā tutlā ʿalayhim āyātunā bayyinātin qāla lladhīna lā yarjūna liqāʾanā iʾti bi-qurʾānin ghayri hādhā aw baddilhu qul mā yakūnu lī an ubaddilahu min tilqāʾi nafsī in attabiʿu illā mā yūḥā ilayya innī akhāfu in ʿaṣaytu rabbī ʿadhāba yawmin ʿaẓīmin
versets.trad: When Our clear verses are recited to them, those who do not hope for Our meeting say: “Bring us a Qurʾān other than this, or alter it!” Say: “It is not for me to alter it of my own accord; I only follow what is revealed to me. Truly I fear, if I disobey my Lord, the chastisement of a tremendous Day!”
versets.commentaire: If they suggest that you bring what We have not commanded you, or show them signs We have not manifested through you, inform them that you do not possess yourself and are not handed over to your own will: We watch over you, We dispose of you. You follow what We bring about in you, without inventing what occurs in you. Such, says Qushayrī, is the posture of the prophet and, at another rank, of the friend of God.

versets.ar: قُل لَّوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ مَا تَلَوْتُهُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَلَا أَدْرَاكُم بِهِ ۖ فَقَدْ لَبِثْتُ فِيكُمْ عُمُرًا مِّن قَبْلِهِ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: qul law shāʾa llāhu mā talawtuhu ʿalaykum wa-lā adrākum bihi fa-qad labithtu fīkum ʿumuran min qablihi a-fa-lā taʿqilūna
versets.trad: Say: “Had God willed, I would not have recited it to you, nor would He have made it known to you through me. I have indeed spent a lifetime among you before this. Will you not understand?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: I have lived among you a lifetime before this; you have known my states, you have found me upright and constant in rectitude. Had God not sent me and charged me, I would never have brought this Law nor recited this Book. “Will you not reason? What have you to object without looking at yourselves?”

mots.racine: ع م ر / ق ب ل

versets.ar: فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرَىٰ عَلَى اللَّهِ كَذِبًا أَوْ كَذَّبَ بِآيَاتِهِ ۚ إِنَّهُ لَا يُفْلِحُ الْمُجْرِمُونَ
versets.tr: fa-man aẓlamu mimmani ftarā ʿalā llāhi kadhiban aw kadhdhaba bi-āyātihi innahu lā yufliḥu l-mujrimūna
versets.trad: Who is more unjust than he who forges a lie against God or denies His signs? Truly the guilty do not prosper.
versets.commentaire: Lying is ugly in every law; when it is against God, it becomes uglier still. Qushayrī notes a dreadful case: among those who forge a lie against God are those who claim spiritual states they have not truly attained — their punishment is to be deprived of them forever, never to reach them again.

mots.racine: ف ر ي / ك ذ ب
mots.sens: to forge a lie against God

versets.ar: وَيَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ مَا لَا يَضُرُّهُمْ وَلَا يَنفَعُهُمْ وَيَقُولُونَ هَٰؤُلَاءِ شُفَعَاؤُنَا عِندَ اللَّهِ ۚ قُلْ أَتُنَبِّئُونَ اللَّهَ بِمَا لَا يَعْلَمُ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَلَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۚ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَىٰ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yaʿbudūna min dūni llāhi mā lā yaḍurruhum wa-lā yanfaʿuhum wa-yaqūlūna hāʾulāʾi shufaʿāʾunā ʿinda llāhi qul a-tunabbiʾūna llāha bi-mā lā yaʿlamu fī l-samāwāti wa-lā fī l-arḍi subḥānahu wa-taʿālā ʿammā yushrikūna
versets.trad: What they worship besides God can neither harm them nor profit them. They say: “These are our intercessors with God!” Say: “Would you inform God of what He does not know in the heavens or on the earth? Glory to Him and exalted is He above what they associate with Him!”
versets.commentaire: He blamed them for worshipping what brings neither harm nor profit. By implication, the true Worshipped is the One from whom harm and profit proceed. Of their extreme dullness, they hope at the end for the intercession of what, in the present instant, produces neither evil nor good. Whoever hangs his heart on creatures to ward off ills and attract goods takes the road of idol-worshippers, for the Creator of all things from nothing to being is God alone.

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ النَّاسُ إِلَّا أُمَّةً وَاحِدَةً فَاخْتَلَفُوا ۚ وَلَوْلَا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِن رَّبِّكَ لَقُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ فِيمَا فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna l-nāsu illā ummatan wāḥidatan fa-khtalafū wa-lawlā kalimatun sabaqat min rabbika la-quḍiya baynahum fīmā fīhi yakhtalifūna
versets.trad: People were but one single community; then they differed. Had a Word from your Lord not gone forth beforehand, judgement would have been passed between them concerning their differences.
versets.commentaire: From Adam until the first conflicts, mankind was but one community. The decree of the True preceded that their reckoning be deferred to the next life; that is why He does not hasten for them the Resurrection they demand. And Qushayrī adds: they only differed because God singled out one people by His solicitude and pleasure, and others by His estrangement; without this, no discord would exist among them.

mots.racine: أ م م / و ح د
ك ل م / س ب ق

versets.ar: وَيَقُولُونَ لَوْلَا أُنزِلَ عَلَيْهِ آيَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِ ۖ فَقُلْ إِنَّمَا الْغَيْبُ لِلَّهِ فَانتَظِرُوا إِنِّي مَعَكُم مِّنَ الْمُنتَظِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-yaqūlūna lawlā unzila ʿalayhi āyatun min rabbihi fa-qul innamā l-ghaybu lillāhi fa-ntaẓirū innī maʿakum mina l-muntaẓirīna
versets.trad: They say: “If only a sign had been sent down to him from his Lord!” Say: “The Unseen belongs to God alone. So wait! Truly I shall wait with you!”
versets.commentaire: He teaches that the Prophet remains, in general, in the veil of the unseen and the cover of the unknown — his knowledge does not reach what is to come, save in the places of election where he is illumined by the lights of gnosis. They await what will happen, and he too awaits what the decree will bring; the difference is that he contemplates what comes through Him and from Him, while they stagger in the valleys of ignorance, ascribing things now to the age, now to the star, now to nature — which is but error and blindness.

mots.def: knowledge of the hidden reserved to Him.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا أَذَقْنَا النَّاسَ رَحْمَةً مِّن بَعْدِ ضَرَّاءَ مَسَّتْهُمْ إِذَا لَهُم مَّكْرٌ فِي آيَاتِنَا ۚ قُلِ اللَّهُ أَسْرَعُ مَكْرًا ۚ إِنَّ رُسُلَنَا يَكْتُبُونَ مَا تَمْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā adhaqnā l-nāsa raḥmatan min baʿdi ḍarrāʾa massat-hum idhā lahum makrun fī āyātinā quli llāhu asraʿu makran inna rusulanā yaktubūna mā tamkurūna
versets.trad: When We let people taste a mercy after some affliction has touched them, behold, they devise stratagems against Our signs. Say: “God is swifter in stratagem, and Our messengers (the angels) are writing down what you devise.”
versets.commentaire: When trial strikes them and We deliver them, they ascribe the thing to other than Us: “We were given rain by such a star,” or “This is the felicity of a star, the effect of a sphere, the bounty of an age”. Such is their stratagem; as for God's stratagem, it is their requital. Qushayrī also applies this to the elite: if the aspirant experiences a veil, then a dévoilement or an approach, his duty is not to pause there, much less settle there; if he does not rise from the contemplation of his states to vanishing in the contemplation of the True, God uses stratagem upon him by dispersing him in those states — such is His stratagem toward His elect.

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي يُسَيِّرُكُمْ فِي الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ ۖ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا كُنتُمْ فِي الْفُلْكِ وَجَرَيْنَ بِهِم بِرِيحٍ طَيِّبَةٍ وَفَرِحُوا بِهَا جَاءَتْهَا رِيحٌ عَاصِفٌ وَجَاءَهُمُ الْمَوْجُ مِن كُلِّ مَكَانٍ وَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُمْ أُحِيطَ بِهِمْ ۙ دَعَوُا اللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ لَئِنْ أَنجَيْتَنَا مِنْ هَٰذِهِ لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الشَّاكِرِينَ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī yusayyirukum fī l-barri wa-l-baḥri ḥattā idhā kuntum fī l-fulki wa-jarayna bihim bi-rīḥin ṭayyibatin wa-fariḥū bihā jāʾat-hā rīḥun ʿāṣifun wa-jāʾahumu l-mawju min kulli makānin wa-ẓannū annahum uḥīṭa bihim daʿawu llāha mukhliṣīna lahu l-dīna laʾin anjaytanā min hādhihi la-nakūnanna mina l-shākirīna
versets.trad: It is He who makes you travel on land and sea. Thus you were aboard ships sailing with a fair wind, and the people rejoiced therein. A violent wind arose and waves assailed them from every side, until they believed themselves engulfed. They called upon God, devoting religion sincerely to Him alone: “If You save us from this, we shall surely be among the thankful!”
versets.commentaire: In the morning, they drag their robes through blessings; at evening, they weep all night; they fall asleep in joy and wake to find that the secrets of the decree have annihilated them. When they return to God in sincere call, He showers them by lifting the trial. Yet hardly has He saved them than they return to other than Him, resuming their rebellious ways. The distress at sea is the image of every distress that strips the heart of its fictitious supports.

mots.racine: خ ل ص / د ي ن

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا أَنجَاهُمْ إِذَا هُمْ يَبْغُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِّ ۗ يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّمَا بَغْيُكُمْ عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِكُم ۖ مَّتَاعَ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا ۖ ثُمَّ إِلَيْنَا مَرْجِعُكُمْ فَنُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā anjāhum idhā hum yabghūna fī l-arḍi bi-ghayri l-ḥaqqi yā ayyuhā l-nāsu innamā baghyukum ʿalā anfusikum matāʿa l-ḥayāti l-dunyā thumma ilaynā marjiʿukum fa-nunabbiʾukum bi-mā kuntum taʿmalūna
versets.trad: Yet hardly had God saved them than they sowed corruption and injustice on earth. O people! Your rebellion turns only against yourselves — a brief enjoyment of the life of this world; then to Us is your return, and We shall inform you of what you used to do.
versets.commentaire: Your rebellion falls back only upon yourselves: We grant you a few days of brief enjoyment, then you shall taste its consequences — a long torment. The word warns that the wrong done to others is, in truth, a wrong done to oneself, and that every passage through this world is counted.

mots.sens: enjoyment of the life of this world

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا مَثَلُ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا كَمَاءٍ أَنزَلْنَاهُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ فَاخْتَلَطَ بِهِ نَبَاتُ الْأَرْضِ مِمَّا يَأْكُلُ النَّاسُ وَالْأَنْعَامُ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا أَخَذَتِ الْأَرْضُ زُخْرُفَهَا وَازَّيَّنَتْ وَظَنَّ أَهْلُهَا أَنَّهُمْ قَادِرُونَ عَلَيْهَا أَتَاهَا أَمْرُنَا لَيْلًا أَوْ نَهَارًا فَجَعَلْنَاهَا حَصِيدًا كَأَن لَّمْ تَغْنَ بِالْأَمْسِ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نُفَصِّلُ الْآيَاتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: innamā mathalu l-ḥayāti l-dunyā ka-māʾin anzalnāhu mina l-samāʾi fa-khtalaṭa bihi nabātu l-arḍi mimmā yaʾkulu l-nāsu wa-l-anʿāmu ḥattā idhā akhadhati l-arḍu zukhrufahā wa-zzayyanat wa-ẓanna ahluhā annahum qādirūna ʿalayhā atāhā amrunā laylan aw nahāran fa-jaʿalnāhā ḥaṣīdan ka-an lam taghna bi-l-amsi kadhālika nufaṣṣilu l-āyāti li-qawmin yatafakkarūna
versets.trad: The life of this world is only like water We send down from the sky: it mingles with the plants of the earth from which people and beasts eat. When the earth has taken on its adornment and decked itself out, and its inhabitants imagine they have power over it, Our command comes upon it by night or by day, and We make of it a harvested field, as though it had not flourished the day before. Thus do We set forth Our signs for people who reflect.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens a long meditation: this world is like water descending from the sky — it makes the earth green, adorns its gardens, and its inhabitants believe they are masters; then the order comes suddenly, and it becomes a harvested field. So too with a human: after maturity, death removes him; his well-ordered affairs unravel. Rain does not come by stratagem — it is asked of God. And: water in its place is life; out of its place, ruin — so too with goods: for the worthy, safeguard; for others, scourge. While it flows, water is pure; stagnant, it spoils — so too goods: spent, they are praised; hoarded, they become blameworthy. And, parable more secret still: like a servant with limpid states, whose branches of intimacy bend and whose gardens of nearness grow green — then an evil eye reaches him, and the branch of his union withers.

mots.racine: ز خ ر ف / ز ي ن

versets.ar: وَاللَّهُ يَدْعُو إِلَىٰ دَارِ السَّلَامِ وَيَهْدِي مَن يَشَاءُ إِلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-llāhu yadʿū ilā dāri l-salāmi wa-yahdī man yashāʾu ilā ṣirāṭin mustaqīmin
versets.trad: God calls to the Abode of Peace and He guides whom He wills to a straight path.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He called them to the Abode of Peace; in reality, He called them to what would earn them access to it — embracing His commands, refraining from His prohibitions. The call belongs to the charge, the elective guidance of His own to honor. The call is by the majesty of His authority; guidance is by the gentleness of His grace. And Dār al-Salām: His name — for Salām is one of His Names; abode of peace, for its inhabitants are safe from the burning and safe from separation: safe from the burning, they reach the delight of His gift; safe from separation, they reach the nobility of His Meeting. The straight path too has degrees: the path of the submitted by certainty of knowledge, the path of the elect by certainty of vision, the path of the beneficent by certainty of the True — according to the hadith: “iḥsān is to worship God as though you saw Him.”

mots.def: Salām: divine Name and state of the elect.

mots.racine: ص ر ط / ق و م

versets.ar: لِّلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا الْحُسْنَىٰ وَزِيَادَةٌ ۖ وَلَا يَرْهَقُ وُجُوهَهُمْ قَتَرٌ وَلَا ذِلَّةٌ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ الْجَنَّةِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: li-lladhīna aḥsanū l-ḥusnā wa-ziyādatun wa-lā yarhaqu wujūhahum qatarun wa-lā dhillatun ulāʾika aṣḥābu l-jannati hum fīhā khālidūna
versets.trad: For those who do the beautiful, the Most Beautiful — and an increase. No dust, no humiliation shall cover their faces. They are the people of Paradise, abiding therein forever.
versets.commentaire: “Those who have done the beautiful”: who have acted according to the divine authorization; who have left no duty undone nor omitted any supererogatory act; for whom no unpaid right remains — toward the True, without shortcoming; toward creatures, without delay. The beautiful reward (al-ḥusnā), according to concordant tradition, is the Vision; the increase (ziyāda) is its perpetual duration. Or: ḥusnā is the Meeting, and ziyāda the permanence in the state of Meeting. No veil of dust shall touch their faces — unlike the deniers whose faces are covered with humiliation.

mots.racine: ح س ن / ز ي د
mots.sens: the most beautiful, and an increase
mots.def: per tradition, the Vision and its eternity.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ كَسَبُوا السَّيِّئَاتِ جَزَاءُ سَيِّئَةٍ بِمِثْلِهَا وَتَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ ۖ مَّا لَهُم مِّنَ اللَّهِ مِنْ عَاصِمٍ ۖ كَأَنَّمَا أُغْشِيَتْ وُجُوهُهُمْ قِطَعًا مِّنَ اللَّيْلِ مُظْلِمًا ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna kasabū l-sayyiʾāti jazāʾu sayyiʾatin bi-mithlihā wa-tarhaquhum dhillatun mā lahum mina llāhi min ʿāṣimin ka-annamā ughshiyat wujūhuhum qiṭaʿan mina l-layli muẓliman ulāʾika aṣḥābu l-nāri hum fīhā khālidūna
versets.trad: As for those who have earned evil deeds, the recompense of an evil deed shall be its like, and humiliation shall cover them. They shall have no protector against God. Their faces shall be as if covered with patches of dark night. Such are the people of the Fire, abiding therein forever.
versets.commentaire: To each evil deed, a like recompense; humiliation covers them — this is the perpetuity of the chastisement. No protector against God: they are marked by the seal of the veil, and the traces of separation are visible on their faces, for the outward betrays the inward.

mots.racine: ق ط ع / ل ي ل

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُهُمْ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ نَقُولُ لِلَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا مَكَانَكُمْ أَنتُمْ وَشُرَكَاؤُكُمْ ۚ فَزَيَّلْنَا بَيْنَهُمْ ۖ وَقَالَ شُرَكَاؤُهُم مَّا كُنتُمْ إِيَّانَا تَعْبُدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yawma naḥshuruhum jamīʿan thumma naqūlu li-lladhīna ashrakū makānakum antum wa-shurakāʾukum fa-zayyalnā baynahum wa-qāla shurakāʾuhum mā kuntum iyyānā taʿbudūna
versets.trad: On a Day We shall gather them all together, then We shall say to the associators: “Stay in your places, you and your associates!” Then We shall separate them one from another. Their associates shall say: “It was not us you used to worship!
versets.commentaire: On the Day of Gathering, God brings forth the deniers with what they have associated with Him. The idols disavow them: “You were not truly worshipping us.” Thus all that was associated with God is revealed empty and elusive.

versets.ar: فَكَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ شَهِيدًا بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَكُمْ إِن كُنَّا عَنْ عِبَادَتِكُمْ لَغَافِلِينَ
versets.tr: fa-kafā bi-llāhi shahīdan baynanā wa-baynakum in kunnā ʿan ʿibādatikum la-ghāfilīna
versets.trad: God suffices as witness between us and you that we were heedless of your worship.”
versets.commentaire: “God suffices as witness between us and you” — the pseudo-deities are declared strangers to all worship; God will make them speak on the Day of Resurrection to confound their worshippers.

mots.racine: ك ف ي / ش ه د

versets.ar: هُنَالِكَ تَبْلُو كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّا أَسْلَفَتْ ۚ وَرُدُّوا إِلَى اللَّهِ مَوْلَاهُمُ الْحَقِّ ۖ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ
versets.tr: hunālika tablū kullu nafsin mā aslafat wa-ruddū ilā llāhi mawlāhumu l-ḥaqqi wa-ḍalla ʿanhum mā kānū yaftarūna
versets.trad: There, every soul shall taste the consequences of its past deeds. All shall be returned to God, their true Master, and what they had invented shall forsake them.
themes: the return to the true Master
versets.commentaire: They will only realize their bankruptcy when they taste the bitterness of their humiliation. Returned to God, they will find only estrangement and rejection — such is the requital of whoever prefers another to Him.

mots.racine: ب ل و / ن ف س
و ل ي / ح ق ق

versets.ar: قُلْ مَن يَرْزُقُكُم مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ وَالْأَرْضِ أَمَّن يَمْلِكُ السَّمْعَ وَالْأَبْصَارَ وَمَن يُخْرِجُ الْحَيَّ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ وَيُخْرِجُ الْمَيِّتَ مِنَ الْحَيِّ وَمَن يُدَبِّرُ الْأَمْرَ ۚ فَسَيَقُولُونَ اللَّهُ ۚ فَقُلْ أَفَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: qul man yarzuqukum mina l-samāʾi wa-l-arḍi am-man yamliku l-samʿa wa-l-abṣāra wa-man yukhriju l-ḥayya mina l-mayyiti wa-yukhriju l-mayyita mina l-ḥayyi wa-man yudabbiru l-amra fa-sa-yaqūlūna llāhu fa-qul a-fa-lā tattaqūna
versets.trad: Say: “Who is it that provides for you from the heavens and the earth? Who has power over hearing and sight? Who brings forth the living from the dead and the dead from the living? And who governs the course of all affairs?” They will say: “God!” Say then: “Will you not fear Him?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: as the True singled Himself out as Creator, He singled Himself out as Provider. Provision is of several orders: for bodies, bread; for hearts, success in obedience or abandonment in fall; for spirits, the realities of union for some, oblivion in this world and reprieve in the next for others. “Who has power over hearing and sight?”: He perfects some sights through Unity, and blinds others to the Real. “They will say: God” — but by conjecture, not by evidence; in word, not by confirmation of the inner.

mots.racine: خ ر ج / ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.sens: He brings forth the living from the dead

versets.ar: فَذَٰلِكُمُ اللَّهُ رَبُّكُمُ الْحَقُّ ۖ فَمَاذَا بَعْدَ الْحَقِّ إِلَّا الضَّلَالُ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ تُصْرَفُونَ
versets.tr: fa-dhālikumu llāhu rabbukumu l-ḥaqqu fa-mādhā baʿda l-ḥaqqi illā l-ḍalālu fa-annā tuṣrafūna
versets.trad: Such is God, your Lord, the Truth! And what is there beyond the Truth save error? How then are you turned away?
versets.commentaire: Beyond the Truth, there is only error. Every object of the Will, every effect of Power is but an empty shell: the decrees of predetermination pass through them.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ح ق ق
mots.def: al-Ḥaqq: divine Name and name of attribute.

versets.ar: كَذَٰلِكَ حَقَّتْ كَلِمَتُ رَبِّكَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ فَسَقُوا أَنَّهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: kadhālika ḥaqqat kalimatu rabbika ʿalā lladhīna fasaqū annahum lā yuʾminūna
versets.trad: Thus has the Word of your Lord come true against those who transgress: they do not believe.
versets.commentaire: The decree preceded them, the Word was confirmed concerning them: no change to His decree, no substitution to His word — secondary causes do not alter what is in Eternity.

mots.racine: ح ق ق / ك ل م
mots.sens: the word of your Lord has come true

versets.ar: قُلْ هَلْ مِن شُرَكَائِكُم مَّن يَبْدَأُ الْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُ ۚ قُلِ اللَّهُ يَبْدَأُ الْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ تُؤْفَكُونَ
versets.tr: qul hal min shurakāʾikum man yabdaʾu l-khalqa thumma yuʿīduhu quli llāhu yabdaʾu l-khalqa thumma yuʿīduhu fa-annā tuʾfakūna
versets.trad: Say: “Is there among your associates one who originates creation, then renews it?” Say: “God originates creation, then renews it. How then are you deluded?”
versets.commentaire: He unveils the ugliness of their belief: they worship what has neither creation nor repetition. The only one worthy of worship is the One from whom invention and renewal proceed. Some associate with Him in origination by claim to power, others deny Him the power to begin again — all incline toward unbelief.

versets.ar: قُلْ هَلْ مِن شُرَكَائِكُم مَّن يَهْدِي إِلَى الْحَقِّ ۚ قُلِ اللَّهُ يَهْدِي لِلْحَقِّ ۗ أَفَمَن يَهْدِي إِلَى الْحَقِّ أَحَقُّ أَن يُتَّبَعَ أَمَّن لَّا يَهِدِّي إِلَّا أَن يُهْدَىٰ ۖ فَمَا لَكُمْ كَيْفَ تَحْكُمُونَ
versets.tr: qul hal min shurakāʾikum man yahdī ilā l-ḥaqqi quli llāhu yahdī li-l-ḥaqqi a-fa-man yahdī ilā l-ḥaqqi aḥaqqu an yuttabaʿa am-man lā yahiddī illā an yuhdā fa-mā lakum kayfa taḥkumūna
versets.trad: Say: “Is there among your associates one who guides to the Truth?” Say: “God guides to the Truth. Is then He who guides to the Truth more worthy to be followed, or he who guides not unless he himself is guided? What is wrong with you that you judge thus?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the True (al-Ḥaqq) is one of His Names — it means the Existing, the Holder of right, the One who establishes the true. The true in the creature is the beautiful act, sound belief, lawful speech. And there is a subtle distinction: he whom the True guides by the True to the True halts there; rare is the one whom the True guides to the True for the True — this one no longer has share or lot, he is wholly consumed in Him.

mots.racine: ه د ي / ح ق ق
ك ي ف / ح ك م

versets.ar: وَمَا يَتَّبِعُ أَكْثَرُهُمْ إِلَّا ظَنًّا ۚ إِنَّ الظَّنَّ لَا يُغْنِي مِنَ الْحَقِّ شَيْئًا ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ بِمَا يَفْعَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā yattabiʿu aktharuhum illā ẓannan inna l-ẓanna lā yughnī mina l-ḥaqqi shayʾan inna llāha ʿalīmun bi-mā yafʿalūna
versets.trad: Most of them follow only conjecture. Yet conjecture avails nothing against the Truth. Truly God knows what they do.
versets.commentaire: Conjecture (ẓann) contradicts certainty: it is to lean toward one side of a judgement without settling. The people of reality are in evidence and settle — conjecture, in the attributes of the True, is blameworthy; the verdict, in the attributes of the soul, is blameworthy for anyone. The servant must, in the instant, empty himself of all conjecture about himself, for none knows the hidden of his becoming; on the other hand, in the attributes of God, he must be in certainty and full vision.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ف ع ل

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ هَٰذَا الْقُرْآنُ أَن يُفْتَرَىٰ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ وَلَٰكِن تَصْدِيقَ الَّذِي بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَتَفْصِيلَ الْكِتَابِ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ مِن رَّبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna hādhā l-qurʾānu an yuftarā min dūni llāhi wa-lākin taṣdīqa lladhī bayna yadayhi wa-tafṣīla l-kitābi lā rayba fīhi min rabbi l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: This Qurʾān was not invented by any besides God; rather, it is the confirmation of what came before it, and the detailed exposition, by the Lord of the Worlds, of the Book in which there is no doubt.
versets.commentaire: Their understandings have been blocked: the multiplication of hearing the Qurʾān only adds blindness upon blindness in them, just as it adds guidance upon guidance in the people of reality. Glory to the One who has made the hearing of His word, for some, cause of bewilderment, and for others, reason of clear-sightedness.

mots.racine: ف ص ل / ك ت ب

versets.ar: أَمْ يَقُولُونَ افْتَرَاهُ ۖ قُلْ فَأْتُوا بِسُورَةٍ مِّثْلِهِ وَادْعُوا مَنِ اسْتَطَعْتُم مِّن دُونِ اللَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: am yaqūlūna ftarāhu qul fa-ʾtū bi-sūratin mithlihi wa-dʿū mani staṭaʿtum min dūni llāhi in kuntum ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: Or do they say: “He has invented it”? Say: “Then bring a sūra like it, and call on whomever you can besides God, if you are truthful.”
versets.commentaire: Spirits have exhausted themselves, the flames of eloquence are extinguished, every renowned orator has acknowledged his impotence to imitate this Book — none has dared try without covering himself in shame.

mots.racine: س و ر / م ث ل

versets.ar: بَلْ كَذَّبُوا بِمَا لَمْ يُحِيطُوا بِعِلْمِهِ وَلَمَّا يَأْتِهِمْ تَأْوِيلُهُ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ كَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۖ فَانظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: bal kadhdhabū bi-mā lam yuḥīṭū bi-ʿilmihi wa-lammā yaʾtihim taʾwīluhu kadhālika kadhdhaba lladhīna min qablihim fa-nẓur kayfa kāna ʿāqibatu l-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: Rather, they deny what their knowledge does not embrace and whose interpretation has not yet come to them. Those before them denied likewise. See then what was the end of the unjust!
versets.commentaire: They met the True with denial, because their knowledge is too short for verification; yet confirmation is conditioned upon verification — only those whose hearts God, by inner tenderness, illumines with the light of evidence and from whom He turns the motives of doubt, believe in the unseen.

mots.racine: ح و ط / ع ل م
mots.sens: they did not embrace its knowledge

mots.racine: ع ق ب / ظ ل م

versets.ar: وَمِنْهُم مَّن يُؤْمِنُ بِهِ وَمِنْهُم مَّن لَّا يُؤْمِنُ بِهِ ۚ وَرَبُّكَ أَعْلَمُ بِالْمُفْسِدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-minhum man yuʾminu bihi wa-minhum man lā yuʾminu bihi wa-rabbuka aʿlamu bi-l-mufsidīna
versets.trad: Among them are some who believe in it and others who do not; but your Lord best knows the corrupters.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: those who have believed are those whose hearts' eyes the True has anointed with the light of certainty; those who have not believed are those whose hearts He has marked with blindness — such is God's custom toward the two groups, and you will find no change to God's custom.

versets.ar: وَإِن كَذَّبُوكَ فَقُل لِّي عَمَلِي وَلَكُمْ عَمَلُكُمْ ۖ أَنتُم بَرِيئُونَ مِمَّا أَعْمَلُ وَأَنَا بَرِيءٌ مِّمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-in kadhdhabūka fa-qul lī ʿamalī wa-lakum ʿamalukum antum barīʾūna mimmā aʿmalu wa-anā barīʾun mimmā taʿmalūna
versets.trad: If they cry lies to you, say: “To me my deeds, and to you your deeds. You are innocent of what I do, and I am innocent of what you do.”
versets.commentaire: The veil is lifted, the realities are clear, the two paths are distinguished: the beneficent is not chastised for the wicked's crime, nor the wicked blamed for the beneficent's virtue — each is judged apart, according to what he does.

mots.sens: to me my work, to you yours

versets.ar: وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَسْتَمِعُونَ إِلَيْكَ ۚ أَفَأَنتَ تُسْمِعُ الصُّمَّ وَلَوْ كَانُوا لَا يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-minhum man yastamiʿūna ilayka a-fa-anta tusmiʿu l-ṣumma wa-law kānū lā yaʿqilūna
versets.trad: Among them are some who listen to you. But can you make the deaf hear, even though they do not understand?
versets.commentaire: Whoever listens by his own strength sinks into his backwardness; whoever listens to the True by the grace of the True needs no effort to understand — the True makes His friends hear what He confides to them in their secrets, and when they hear the call of the Mediator (the Prophet), they receive it. As for the one whom God does not make hear from the side of comprehension, listening to people only adds denial upon denial in him.

mots.racine: س م ع / ص م م

versets.ar: وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَنظُرُ إِلَيْكَ ۚ أَفَأَنتَ تَهْدِي الْعُمْيَ وَلَوْ كَانُوا لَا يُبْصِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-minhum man yanẓuru ilayka a-fa-anta tahdī l-ʿumya wa-law kānū lā yubṣirūna
versets.trad: And among them are some who look at you. But can you guide the blind, even though they do not see?
versets.commentaire: Whoever looks without looking at God through God adds only veil upon veil — “it is not the eyes that go blind, but the hearts that are within the breasts”. And according to the divine hadith: “through Me he hears, through Me he sees”. The poet: “Contemplate, if you know how to look, by the eye of the True, a sight that from Him returns to Him.”

mots.racine: ه د ي / ع م ي

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَظْلِمُ النَّاسَ شَيْئًا وَلَٰكِنَّ النَّاسَ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: inna llāha lā yaẓlimu l-nāsa shayʾan wa-lākinna l-nāsa anfusahum yaẓlimūna
versets.trad: God does not wrong people in any way, but it is people who wrong themselves.
versets.commentaire: He has stripped Himself of what is impossible to imagine concerning Him. How would He qualify Himself with injustice, when everything one could conceive belongs to Him? The right is His right, the Kingdom is His Kingdom — of whom no evil can issue, how could He be qualified with injustice?

mots.racine: ن ف س / ظ ل م

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ يَحْشُرُهُمْ كَأَن لَّمْ يَلْبَثُوا إِلَّا سَاعَةً مِّنَ النَّهَارِ يَتَعَارَفُونَ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ قَدْ خَسِرَ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِلِقَاءِ اللَّهِ وَمَا كَانُوا مُهْتَدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-yawma yaḥshuruhum ka-an lam yalbathū illā sāʿatan mina l-nahāri yataʿārafūna baynahum qad khasira lladhīna kadhdhabū bi-liqāʾi llāhi wa-mā kānū muhtadīna
versets.trad: The Day He gathers them, it shall be as though they had not lingered [in their graves] but an hour of the day, and they shall recognize one another. Those who denied the meeting with God will have lost, and they were not rightly guided.
versets.commentaire: Days and months, years and centuries, after their passing, are at the measure of an instant for whoever pauses on them — what trace do they keep after their dissipation? The to-come is near, and the measure of the past is as if it had never been. Such is the illusion of time for those who have denied the Meeting.

mots.sens: as if they had not lingered

versets.ar: وَإِمَّا نُرِيَنَّكَ بَعْضَ الَّذِي نَعِدُهُمْ أَوْ نَتَوَفَّيَنَّكَ فَإِلَيْنَا مَرْجِعُهُمْ ثُمَّ اللَّهُ شَهِيدٌ عَلَىٰ مَا يَفْعَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-immā nuriyannaka baʿḍa lladhī naʿiduhum aw natawaffayannaka fa-ilaynā marjiʿuhum thumma llāhu shahīdun ʿalā mā yafʿalūna
versets.trad: Whether We show you a part of what We promise them, or take you to Us before that, to Us is their return; then God is witness over what they do.
versets.commentaire: His announcement is true, His promise and threat are truth; after the unfolding comes the gathering, then the demand of accounts and recompense — how swift the known becomes contemplated and present!

versets.ar: وَلِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَّسُولٌ ۖ فَإِذَا جَاءَ رَسُولُهُمْ قُضِيَ بَيْنَهُم بِالْقِسْطِ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-li-kulli ummatin rasūlun fa-idhā jāʾa rasūluhum quḍiya baynahum bi-l-qisṭi wa-hum lā yuẓlamūna
versets.trad: For every community there is a messenger. When their messenger comes, judgement is passed among them with equity, and they are not wronged.
versets.commentaire: He has left no time without Law, no Law without judgement, no judgement without reward and chastisement that follow it. Such is the divine economy of justice through the history of nations.

mots.racine: أ م م / ر س ل

versets.ar: وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَٰذَا الْوَعْدُ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-yaqūlūna matā hādhā l-waʿdu in kuntum ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: They say: “When shall this promise be fulfilled, if you are truthful?”
versets.commentaire: Hastening to see the Promise fall is the mark of deniers. The people of reality, on the contrary, before the coming of a burden have no preoccupation, nor haste at the moment of its being, nor willed acceptance of what it entails — they are thrown into the prison of the decree, and no vein beats in them by their own choice.

versets.ar: قُل لَّا أَمْلِكُ لِنَفْسِي ضَرًّا وَلَا نَفْعًا إِلَّا مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ ۗ لِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلٌ ۚ إِذَا جَاءَ أَجَلُهُمْ فَلَا يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ سَاعَةً ۖ وَلَا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ
versets.tr: qul lā amliku li-nafsī ḍarran wa-lā nafʿan illā mā shāʾa llāhu li-kulli ummatin ajalun idhā jāʾa ajaluhum fa-lā yastaʾkhirūna sāʿatan wa-lā yastaqdimūna
versets.trad: Say: “I possess no power over my own harm or benefit, save what God wills.” For every community there is an appointed term. When their term comes, they cannot delay it by an hour, nor advance it.
versets.commentaire: How would the slave possess anything? If the lord of creatures possesses for himself neither harm nor benefit, how would the one whose rank is lower possess a particle or choose an atom? What was not has perished in reality, and the All-Powerful has singled Himself out by the attribute of Sovereignty.

mots.racine: م ل ك / ن ف س

versets.ar: قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ إِنْ أَتَاكُمْ عَذَابُهُ بَيَاتًا أَوْ نَهَارًا مَّاذَا يَسْتَعْجِلُ مِنْهُ الْمُجْرِمُونَ
versets.tr: qul a-raʾaytum in atākum ʿadhābuhu bayātan aw nahāran mādhā yastaʿjilu minhu l-mujrimūna
versets.trad: Say: “Have you considered? If His chastisement should come upon you by night or by day, what part of it would the guilty hasten?”
versets.commentaire: Whoever knows the perfection of Power never believes himself safe from the brutal seizure; whoever fears the nocturnal visit does not delight in sleep. Whoever pillows himself on heedlessness is awakened by the sudden eruption of chastisement; whoever settles on the mount of fault stumbles into the pit of trial.

mots.racine: ب ي ت / ن ه ر

versets.ar: أَثُمَّ إِذَا مَا وَقَعَ آمَنتُم بِهِ ۚ آلْآنَ وَقَدْ كُنتُم بِهِ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ
versets.tr: a-thumma idhā mā waqaʿa āmantum bihi āl-āna wa-qad kuntum bihi tastaʿjilūna
versets.trad: Will you then believe in it only when it has befallen, while now you call for it to be hastened?”
versets.commentaire: After the veil of the unseen has been torn, the supplication of excuses is no longer received; no argument holds after the lifting of pretexts, no excuse after the clarity of proof.

mots.sens: you used to ask for haste

versets.ar: ثُمَّ قِيلَ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ذُوقُوا عَذَابَ الْخُلْدِ هَلْ تُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْسِبُونَ
versets.tr: thumma qīla li-lladhīna ẓalamū dhūqū ʿadhāba l-khuldi hal tujzawna illā bi-mā kuntum taksibūna
versets.trad: Then it shall be said to those who were unjust: “Taste the everlasting chastisement! Are you requited for anything other than what you used to earn?”
versets.commentaire: No soul is charged with anything but to swallow what it has itself poured; no sower harvests but what he has sown. The recompense is the exact echo of the work.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / خ ل د

versets.ar: وَيَسْتَنبِئُونَكَ أَحَقٌّ هُوَ ۖ قُلْ إِي وَرَبِّي إِنَّهُ لَحَقٌّ ۖ وَمَا أَنتُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ
versets.tr: wa-yastanbiʾūnaka a-ḥaqqun huwa qul ī wa-rabbī innahu la-ḥaqqun wa-mā antum bi-muʿjizīna
versets.trad: They will ask you: “Is this true?” Say: “Yes, by my Lord, it is indeed true, and you cannot frustrate the Power of God!”
versets.commentaire: He affirms by oath what the question implies, lifting equivocation for their ignorant; but your warning will not avail them, and your preaching will not touch them — how could it? They have been given to drink of the drink of the veil, branded with the iron of separation: no clear-sightedness, no intelligence, no finesse.

mots.def: iʿjāz: incapacity of others to prevent.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ أَنَّ لِكُلِّ نَفْسٍ ظَلَمَتْ مَا فِي الْأَرْضِ لَافْتَدَتْ بِهِ ۗ وَأَسَرُّوا النَّدَامَةَ لَمَّا رَأَوُا الْعَذَابَ ۖ وَقُضِيَ بَيْنَهُم بِالْقِسْطِ ۚ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law anna li-kulli nafsin ẓalamat mā fī l-arḍi la-ftadat bihi wa-asarrū l-nadāmata lammā raʾawu l-ʿadhāba wa-quḍiya baynahum bi-l-qisṭi wa-hum lā yuẓlamūna
versets.trad: Every soul laden with iniquity, if it possessed all the riches of the earth, would offer them as ransom. They will conceal their regret when they see the chastisement. But it shall be judged among them with equity, and they shall not be wronged.
versets.commentaire: No equivalent will be accepted from them, no excess — no denial of the prior threat. No regret will avail them, however sincere; no dignity will be granted, however demanded; no injustice is wrought upon them. Nay: it is God the Just in His judgement, the One in His altitude by the attribute of His majesty.

mots.sens: it would ransom itself with it

versets.ar: أَلَا إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۗ أَلَا إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقٌّ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: a-lā inna lillāhi mā fī l-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi a-lā inna waʿda llāhi ḥaqqun wa-lākinna aktharahum lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: Truly to God belongs all that is in the heavens and on the earth. The promise of God is Truth, but most people do not know.
versets.commentaire: Events in their totality belong to God as Kingdom; they manifest through Him, proceed from Him, return to Him. His word is truth, His promise truthful, His command peremptory, His decree without appeal — He is the Most High, the Powerful over what He wills.

mots.racine: و ع د / ح ق ق
mots.sens: the promise of God is truth

versets.ar: هُوَ يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: huwa yuḥyī wa-yumītu wa-ilayhi turjaʿūna
versets.trad: It is He who gives life and gives death, and to Him you shall return.
versets.commentaire: He vivifies hearts by the lights of contemplation, and brings death to souls by the various inner struggles (mujāhadāt) — the souls of the worshippers are enveloped by the efforts, the hearts of the gnostics honored by the fountains of contemplation. And: He vivifies whoever turns toward Him, brings death upon whoever turns away from Him; He vivifies some hearts by hope, brings death to others by the seal of despair.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.sens: He gives life and gives death
mots.def: two opposed acts of the sole Master.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ قَدْ جَاءَتْكُم مَّوْعِظَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَشِفَاءٌ لِّمَا فِي الصُّدُورِ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā l-nāsu qad jāʾatkum mawʿiẓatun min rabbikum wa-shifāʾun li-mā fī l-ṣudūri wa-hudan wa-raḥmatun li-l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: O people! There has come to you an admonition from your Lord, a healing for what is in the breasts, a Guidance and a Mercy for the believers.
versets.commentaire: The admonition is addressed to all, but it does not act on some and profits others: whoever lends the ear of his inner self, the light of verification is kindled in his heart; whoever listens to it in his absence remains in the permanence of the veil. And Qushayrī unfolds: the healing of each goes by the measure of his sickness — the healing of sinners is in mercy, that of the obedient in grace, that of the gnostics in nearness, that of the lovers in the very vision of Reality.

mots.racine: ش ف ي / ص د ر
mots.sens: healing for what is in the breasts

versets.ar: قُلْ بِفَضْلِ اللَّهِ وَبِرَحْمَتِهِ فَبِذَٰلِكَ فَلْيَفْرَحُوا هُوَ خَيْرٌ مِّمَّا يَجْمَعُونَ
versets.tr: qul bi-faḍli llāhi wa-bi-raḥmatihi fa-bi-dhālika fa-l-yafraḥū huwa khayrun mimmā yajmaʿūna
versets.trad: Say: “In the Grace of God and in His Mercy — in that let them rejoice! It is better than what they amass!”
versets.commentaire: Grace (faḍl) is non-obligatory beneficence, mercy (raḥma) the will of the benefit. Qushayrī unfolds seven pairings: God's grace is what He has granted them of blessings; His mercy is what He has averted from them of afflictions; grace is what He has honored them with of obediences He has made them practice; mercy is what He has guarded them from of sins they would have committed; grace is the gnosis at the beginning; mercy is the forgiveness at the end; grace is His establishing you in the contemplation of the quest; mercy is His making you contemplate His right by the sentence of evidence until you see Him tomorrow by the dévoilement of vision. “Let them rejoice in that”: by what He has made them worthy of, not by what they impose on themselves by their own movements.

mots.racine: ف ض ل / ر ح م

versets.ar: قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُم مَّا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ لَكُم مِّن رِّزْقٍ فَجَعَلْتُم مِّنْهُ حَرَامًا وَحَلَالًا قُلْ آللَّهُ أَذِنَ لَكُمْ ۖ أَمْ عَلَى اللَّهِ تَفْتَرُونَ
versets.tr: qul a-raʾaytum mā anzala llāhu lakum min rizqin fa-jaʿaltum minhu ḥarāman wa-ḥalālan qul āllāhu adhina lakum am ʿalā llāhi taftarūna
versets.trad: Say: “Have you considered what God has sent down to you for your sustenance? You have declared some of it unlawful and some lawful.” Say: “Has God permitted you, or do you invent lies against Him?”
themes: the invention of lawful and unlawful
versets.commentaire: He blames and reproves them for what they have innovated in authorizations and prohibitions, exposing their lie in what they have ascribed of this to a permission and a Law.

mots.racine: ح ر م / ح ل ل

versets.ar: وَمَا ظَنُّ الَّذِينَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ الْكَذِبَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى النَّاسِ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā ẓannu lladhīna yaftarūna ʿalā llāhi l-kadhiba yawma l-qiyāmati inna llāha la-dhū faḍlin ʿalā l-nāsi wa-lākinna aktharahum lā yashkurūna
versets.trad: What will those who invent lies against God think on the Day of Resurrection? Truly God is full of Grace toward people, but most of them are not thankful.
versets.commentaire: Solemn reproach for the mass of lies they have accumulated. Then: “God is full of grace toward people” — by the reprieve granted to the guilty, and by the preservation granted to the one who has not committed the crime.

versets.ar: وَمَا تَكُونُ فِي شَأْنٍ وَمَا تَتْلُو مِنْهُ مِن قُرْآنٍ وَلَا تَعْمَلُونَ مِنْ عَمَلٍ إِلَّا كُنَّا عَلَيْكُمْ شُهُودًا إِذْ تُفِيضُونَ فِيهِ ۚ وَمَا يَعْزُبُ عَن رَّبِّكَ مِن مِّثْقَالِ ذَرَّةٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ وَلَا أَصْغَرَ مِن ذَٰلِكَ وَلَا أَكْبَرَ إِلَّا فِي كِتَابٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: wa-mā takūnu fī shaʾnin wa-mā tatlū minhu min qurʾānin wa-lā taʿmalūna min ʿamalin illā kunnā ʿalaykum shuhūdan idh tufīḍūna fīhi wa-mā yaʿzubu ʿan rabbika min mithqāli dharratin fī l-arḍi wa-lā fī l-samāʾi wa-lā aṣghara min dhālika wa-lā akbara illā fī kitābin mubīnin
versets.trad: You shall never engage in any matter, nor recite any passage of the Qurʾān, nor shall you perform any deed, but that We are witness over you at the very moment you are immersed in it. Not even the weight of an atom escapes your Lord, whether on earth or in the heavens; nothing smaller than that, nor greater, but is inscribed in a clear Book.
versets.commentaire: He warns them by what He makes them know of His gaze upon them in all their states, and His vision of all the acts they shall do. Knowing that He sees them produces modesty (ḥayāʾ) before Him — such is the station of murāqaba: when the servant knows his Master sees him, he feels shame before Him, abandons the pursuit of his passion, no longer prowls around what He has forbidden. The poet: “It is as if a sentinel from You stood over my soul: if I tried to loosen the matter, it hardened upon me.”

mots.racine: ث ق ل / ذ ر ر

versets.ar: أَلَا إِنَّ أَوْلِيَاءَ اللَّهِ لَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
versets.tr: a-lā inna awliyāʾa llāhi lā khawfun ʿalayhim wa-lā hum yaḥzanūna
versets.trad: Truly, the friends of God shall feel no fear, nor shall they grieve:
versets.commentaire: Walī, on the pattern faʿīl, is an intensification of the agent: one whose acts of obedience have followed one another without any disobedience interrupting them. It can also be faʿīl in the passive sense (like jarīḥ “wounded,” qatīl “killed”), meaning: one upon whom God's beneficence and favors succeed one another, one preserved in all his states from trials — and the most severe trial is to fall into sin: the True preserves him through the continuity of his times from falls. Distinction between the maʿṣūm (prophet, infallible) and the maḥfūẓ (preserved friend): the maʿṣūm never grazes a sin; the maḥfūẓ may know some faults, sometimes some falls, but without persistence. As for “no fear nor grief”: fear = apprehension of an unknown future loss; grief = heaviness on the instant — yet the elite, under the reign of the instant, has no leaning toward the future, and bathes in the breeze of contentment with all that happens; no weight of the moment reaches them.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَكَانُوا يَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna āmanū wa-kānū yattaqūna
versets.trad: for those who believe in God and are mindful of Him —
versets.commentaire: Such is the description of the friends: they had faith in the instant and guarded themselves from association at the final term. And: “they believed”: they stood by their hearts in the gnoses; “and they used to fear”: they stood upright by their souls in the accomplishment of the charges.

versets.ar: لَهُمُ الْبُشْرَىٰ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ ۚ لَا تَبْدِيلَ لِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ
versets.tr: lahumu l-bushrā fī l-ḥayāti l-dunyā wa-fī l-ākhirati lā tabdīla li-kalimāti llāhi dhālika huwa l-fawzu l-ʿaẓīmu
versets.trad: for them is the good tidings in this life and in the Hereafter. The words of God do not change: this is the supreme triumph.
versets.commentaire: When they stand by command and stand upright by abandoning the prohibitions, the Law announces to them the exit from every obligation; the Reality announces to them the meriting of honor, through what is unveiled to them of teachings. Such is the glad tiding in the present. And beyond: “Their Lord gives them glad tidings of a mercy from Him and a pleasure”. Qushayrī adds: the greatest announcement is what they find in their hearts: the victory over themselves by the fall of their cravings — and what reign more perfect than the fall of cravings and contentment with what is?

mots.racine: ف و ز / ع ظ م

versets.ar: وَلَا يَحْزُنكَ قَوْلُهُمْ ۘ إِنَّ الْعِزَّةَ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعًا ۚ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-lā yaḥzunka qawluhum inna l-ʿizzata lillāhi jamīʿan huwa l-samīʿu l-ʿalīmu
versets.trad: Let not their saying grieve you. All might belongs to God alone. He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: So long as the servant is dispersed, his breast tightens and his heart is choked by what he hears from the deniers — things from which the attribute of the True is purified. But if he becomes ʿārif, this attribute leaves him by the verification that the True is beyond all obedience and all fall: neither strangeness nor familiarity come to Him from this. Then the ʿārif verifies that it is God who makes obedience spring from the concordant and who produces the states of the discordant — “the True is not concerned with what passes, and the servant is not concerned with contemplating what passes”.

mots.racine: س م ع / ع ل م

versets.ar: أَلَا إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَن فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَن فِي الْأَرْضِ ۗ وَمَا يَتَّبِعُ الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ شُرَكَاءَ ۚ إِن يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا الظَّنَّ وَإِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَخْرُصُونَ
versets.tr: a-lā inna lillāhi man fī l-samāwāti wa-man fī l-arḍi wa-mā yattabiʿu lladhīna yadʿūna min dūni llāhi shurakāʾa in yattabiʿūna illā l-ẓanna wa-in hum illā yakhruṣūna
versets.trad: Does not all that is in the heavens and on the earth belong to God? What then do those follow who call upon associates besides God? They follow only conjectures and do but guess.
versets.commentaire: To God belongs whoever is in the heavens and on the earth as Kingdom, and He disposes of them as He wills, in cutting judgement. No cause to His pleasure, no motive to His refusal — these are prior decrees, not caused by later faults nor by sincere obediences and worships.

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ اللَّيْلَ لِتَسْكُنُوا فِيهِ وَالنَّهَارَ مُبْصِرًا ۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَسْمَعُونَ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī jaʿala lakumu l-layla li-taskunū fīhi wa-l-nahāra mubṣiran inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-qawmin yasmaʿūna
versets.trad: It is He who made for you the night that you might rest in it, and the day giving sight. Truly in that are signs for a people who hear.
versets.commentaire: The night, for the heedless, is distance and absence; for the contrite, repentance and return; for the lovers, nearness and intimacy. Seemingly unwelcoming, it is the hour of nearness for the people of union — “how many hands the dark night has stretched out to me, attesting that the Manichaeans lie”.

mots.sens: that you may rest in it

versets.ar: قَالُوا اتَّخَذَ اللَّهُ وَلَدًا ۗ سُبْحَانَهُ ۖ هُوَ الْغَنِيُّ ۖ لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۚ إِنْ عِندَكُم مِّن سُلْطَانٍ بِهَٰذَا ۚ أَتَقُولُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: qālū ttakhadha llāhu waladan subḥānahu huwa l-ghaniyyu lahu mā fī l-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi in ʿindakum min sulṭānin bi-hādhā a-taqūlūna ʿalā llāhi mā lā taʿlamūna
versets.trad: They have said: “God has taken a child.” Glory to Him! He is the Self-Sufficient! To Him belongs all that is in the heavens and on the earth. Have you any authority for this? Or do you say about God what you do not know?
versets.commentaire: The child is part of the parent; the ṣamadiyya (divine Plenitude) rises above any parceling — God declares Himself above this with “Glory to Him.” And: “He is the Self-Sufficient” — self-sufficiency is the negation of all need; the desire to procreate is a need, and He rises above this.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / و ل د

versets.ar: قُلْ إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ الْكَذِبَ لَا يُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: qul inna lladhīna yaftarūna ʿalā llāhi l-kadhiba lā yufliḥūna
versets.trad: Say: “Those who invent lies against God shall not prosper.”
versets.commentaire: No success for whoever forges a lie against God — neither in this world the inner opening, nor in the other the deliverance.

versets.ar: مَتَاعٌ فِي الدُّنْيَا ثُمَّ إِلَيْنَا مَرْجِعُهُمْ ثُمَّ نُذِيقُهُمُ الْعَذَابَ الشَّدِيدَ بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْفُرُونَ
versets.tr: matāʿun fī l-dunyā thumma ilaynā marjiʿuhum thumma nudhīquhumu l-ʿadhāba l-shadīda bi-mā kānū yakfurūna
versets.trad: They shall have a brief enjoyment in this world, then to Us shall they return, and We shall make them taste the severe chastisement for their disbelief.
versets.commentaire: They have no true enjoyment in what they are: a few brief days, then long sorrows follow — no rising after that, no useful remorse.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ش د د

versets.ar: وَاتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ نُوحٍ إِذْ قَالَ لِقَوْمِهِ يَا قَوْمِ إِن كَانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكُم مَّقَامِي وَتَذْكِيرِي بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ فَعَلَى اللَّهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ فَأَجْمِعُوا أَمْرَكُمْ وَشُرَكَاءَكُمْ ثُمَّ لَا يَكُنْ أَمْرُكُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ غُمَّةً ثُمَّ اقْضُوا إِلَيَّ وَلَا تُنظِرُونِ
versets.tr: wa-tlu ʿalayhim nabaʾa nūḥin idh qāla li-qawmihi yā qawmi in kāna kabura ʿalaykum maqāmī wa-tadhkīrī bi-āyāti llāhi fa-ʿalā llāhi tawakkaltu fa-ajmiʿū amrakum wa-shurakāʾakum thumma lā yakun amrukum ʿalaykum ghummatan thumma qḍū ilayya wa-lā tunẓirūni
versets.trad: Recite to them the tale of Noah, when he said to his people: “O my people! If my standing among you and my reminder of the signs of God weigh heavily upon you, then on God do I rely. Settle then upon your purpose and your associates, that your affair may not be a worry to you; then carry it out upon me, and grant me no respite!
versets.commentaire: God sent down this verse to console the Prophet for the hardships he endured from his people: the days of Noah, however long, vanished before long. And Qushayrī notes a subtle distinction: Noah says “I rely upon God” — this is the eye of separation (ʿayn al-tafriqa); to Muḥammad it is said “God suffices you” — this is the eye of union (ʿayn al-jamʿ). Thus the Muhammadan eminence is manifested and his singularity appears.

versets.ar: فَإِن تَوَلَّيْتُمْ فَمَا سَأَلْتُكُم مِّنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِيَ إِلَّا عَلَى اللَّهِ ۖ وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-in tawallaytum fa-mā saʾaltukum min ajrin in ajriya illā ʿalā llāhi wa-umirtu an akūna mina l-muslimīna
versets.trad: If you turn away, [remember that] I have asked of you no reward. My reward is only with God, and I have been commanded to be among those who submit (to Him: muslimūn).”
versets.commentaire: When the work is for God, its author asks his wage from none other than God — such is the divine custom in all the friends of God.

versets.ar: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَنَجَّيْنَاهُ وَمَن مَّعَهُ فِي الْفُلْكِ وَجَعَلْنَاهُمْ خَلَائِفَ وَأَغْرَقْنَا الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا ۖ فَانظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الْمُنذَرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-kadhdhabūhu fa-najjaynāhu wa-man maʿahu fī l-fulki wa-jaʿalnāhum khalāʾifa wa-aghraqnā lladhīna kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā fa-nẓur kayfa kāna ʿāqibatu l-mundharīna
versets.trad: They cried lies to him, and We saved him then in the ark, he and those with him. We made them successors, and We drowned those who denied Our signs. See then what was the end of those who had been warned.
versets.commentaire: He drowned his people by the waves of droplets; in reality, He drowned them by the waves of decree and Power, and He safeguarded Noah and his people in the ship — in reality, He saved them in the ship of Safekeeping. Noah was, in His prior decree, among the preserved; his people, in His ancient verdict, among the drowned — the states ran according to what predetermination ran in Eternity.

mots.def: those to whom announcement was made.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَا مِن بَعْدِهِ رُسُلًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِمْ فَجَاءُوهُم بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ فَمَا كَانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا بِمَا كَذَّبُوا بِهِ مِن قَبْلُ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَطْبَعُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِ الْمُعْتَدِينَ
versets.tr: thumma baʿathnā min baʿdihi rusulan ilā qawmihim fa-jāʾūhum bi-l-bayyināti fa-mā kānū li-yuʾminū bi-mā kadhdhabū bihi min qablu kadhālika naṭbaʿu ʿalā qulūbi l-muʿtadīna
versets.trad: Then We sent after him messengers to their peoples, and they came to them with clear proofs. But they would not believe in what they had previously denied. Thus do We set a seal on the hearts of transgressors.
versets.commentaire: The later peoples were not disposed to believe in what they had previously denied; thus God seals the hearts of the transgressors — early denial engenders final hardening.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَا مِن بَعْدِهِم مُّوسَىٰ وَهَارُونَ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَئِهِ بِآيَاتِنَا فَاسْتَكْبَرُوا وَكَانُوا قَوْمًا مُّجْرِمِينَ
versets.tr: thumma baʿathnā min baʿdihim mūsā wa-hārūna ilā firʿawna wa-malaʾihi bi-āyātinā fa-stakbarū wa-kānū qawman mujrimīna
versets.trad: Then We sent after them Moses and Aaron with Our signs to Pharaoh and the notables of his entourage; but they grew proud and were a guilty people.
versets.commentaire: He recounted to the Prophet the announcements of the Ancients and unfolded to him all the states of the departed, then He raised him above them all — they were stars, and he the full moon; they were rivers, and he the ocean; through him their necklace was knotted, through his light their day was illuminated, through his appearance their number was sealed.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا جَاءَهُمُ الْحَقُّ مِنْ عِندِنَا قَالُوا إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَسِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: fa-lammā jāʾahumu l-ḥaqqu min ʿindinā qālū inna hādhā la-siḥrun mubīnun
versets.trad: When the Truth came to them from Us, they exclaimed: “This is manifest magic!”
versets.commentaire: The more the True gave them evidence, the more they swelled in rebellion. Such is God's usage toward those who are removed from His knowledge: He does not increase the proofs of guidance without increasing in their hearts the blindness — the designs of the prophets remain veiled to them.

versets.ar: قَالَ مُوسَىٰ أَتَقُولُونَ لِلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَاءَكُمْ ۖ أَسِحْرٌ هَٰذَا وَلَا يُفْلِحُ السَّاحِرُونَ
versets.tr: qāla mūsā a-taqūlūna li-l-ḥaqqi lammā jāʾakum a-siḥrun hādhā wa-lā yufliḥu l-sāḥirūna
versets.trad: Moses said: “Do you speak thus of the Truth when it has come to you? Is this magic? But magicians do not prosper!”
themes: the magic that does not prosper
versets.commentaire: Moses replies: “Would you call magic the Truth when it has come to you? Yet magicians never prosper” — their art is void of duration.

mots.racine: ف ل ح / س ح ر

versets.ar: قَالُوا أَجِئْتَنَا لِتَلْفِتَنَا عَمَّا وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهِ آبَاءَنَا وَتَكُونَ لَكُمَا الْكِبْرِيَاءُ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَمَا نَحْنُ لَكُمَا بِمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: qālū a-jiʾtanā li-talfitanā ʿammā wajadnā ʿalayhi ābāʾanā wa-takūna lakumā l-kibriyāʾu fī l-arḍi wa-mā naḥnu lakumā bi-muʾminīna
versets.trad: They said: “Have you come to us to turn us away from what we found our fathers upon, that the dominion on earth might belong to you two? We have no trust in you!”
versets.commentaire: They leaned on the imitation of their fathers, loved to prolong what they were in — the evil fate of belief and the bad road reached them, even making them imagine that the prophets were calling to God only to arrogate to themselves greatness over the servants of God — not knowing that they called to it only by the command of God.

mots.racine: و ج د / أ ب و
mots.sens: what we found our fathers upon

versets.ar: وَقَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ ائْتُونِي بِكُلِّ سَاحِرٍ عَلِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-qāla firʿawnu ʾtūnī bi-kulli sāḥirin ʿalīmin
versets.trad: Pharaoh said: “Bring me every expert magician!”
versets.commentaire: When Pharaoh sought help in other than God to push back what was presenting itself, it was not long before he disavowed them and threatened them — such is the end of every alliance or tutelage not in God: it ends in enmity — “Friends, on that Day, will be enemies one to another”.

mots.racine: س ح ر / ع ل م

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا جَاءَ السَّحَرَةُ قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰ أَلْقُوا مَا أَنتُم مُّلْقُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā jāʾa l-saḥaratu qāla lahum mūsā alqū mā antum mulqūna
versets.trad: When the magicians came, Moses said to them: “Cast what you have to cast!”
versets.commentaire: Moses commands them with an order by which their vanity will appear, so that the True may enter upon what they have brought of deception. It is the divine art of letting the false break out by letting the false unfold.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا أَلْقَوْا قَالَ مُوسَىٰ مَا جِئْتُم بِهِ السِّحْرُ ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ سَيُبْطِلُهُ ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُصْلِحُ عَمَلَ الْمُفْسِدِينَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā alqaw qāla mūsā mā jiʾtum bihi l-siḥru inna llāha sa-yubṭiluhu inna llāha lā yuṣliḥu ʿamala l-mufsidīna
versets.trad: When they had cast, Moses said: “What you have brought is magic: God will show its vanity. God does not prosper the work of the corrupters.
versets.commentaire: “God will annul it”: when Moses's staff swallowed all they had brought of ropes and staves — God having transformed it into a serpent — they knew that God had annulled those entities and made them disappear.

versets.ar: وَيُحِقُّ اللَّهُ الْحَقَّ بِكَلِمَاتِهِ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْمُجْرِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yuḥiqqu llāhu l-ḥaqqa bi-kalimātihi wa-law kariha l-mujrimūna
versets.trad: God, by His words, establishes the Truth, however much the guilty resent it!”
versets.commentaire: Among what He has established: the magicians had sworn “by the might of Pharaoh, it is we who shall be the victors!” And the True said, glorified be He: “By My majesty, it is you who shall be the vanquished!” It was according to what the True said, not according to what they said.

versets.ar: فَمَا آمَنَ لِمُوسَىٰ إِلَّا ذُرِّيَّةٌ مِّن قَوْمِهِ عَلَىٰ خَوْفٍ مِّن فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَئِهِمْ أَن يَفْتِنَهُمْ ۚ وَإِنَّ فِرْعَوْنَ لَعَالٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَإِنَّهُ لَمِنَ الْمُسْرِفِينَ
versets.tr: fa-mā āmana li-mūsā illā dhurriyyatun min qawmihi ʿalā khawfin min firʿawna wa-malaʾihim an yaftinahum wa-inna firʿawna la-ʿālin fī l-arḍi wa-innahu la-mina l-musrifīna
versets.trad: None believed in Moses save a posterity of his people, the rest fearing to be persecuted by Pharaoh and by their own chiefs. For Pharaoh was arrogant on the earth and was among the transgressors.
versets.commentaire: The people of reality, in every time, are few in number, great in worth with God. Such is the condition of the faithful little remnant, under every oppression.

mots.racine: ذ ر ر / ق و م

versets.ar: وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰ يَا قَوْمِ إِن كُنتُمْ آمَنتُم بِاللَّهِ فَعَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلُوا إِن كُنتُم مُّسْلِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla mūsā yā qawmi in kuntum āmantum bi-llāhi fa-ʿalayhi tawakkalū in kuntum muslimīna
versets.trad: Moses said: “O my people! If you believe in God, then rely upon Him, if you are submitted to Him.”
versets.commentaire: He teaches that faith is not in mere words: there must necessarily be the sincerity of states (ṣidq al-aḥwāl) — that is intention. And the reality of tawakkul is a mediation continually offered, then one knows that it is by the grace of God, glorified be He, that deliverance is realized — not by what one brings of effort. Such is the truth of tawakkul.

versets.ar: فَقَالُوا عَلَى اللَّهِ تَوَكَّلْنَا رَبَّنَا لَا تَجْعَلْنَا فِتْنَةً لِّلْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-qālū ʿalā llāhi tawakkalnā rabbanā lā tajʿalnā fitnatan li-l-qawmi l-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: They said: “Upon God we rely! Our Lord, do not make us a trial for the wrongdoing people, and
versets.commentaire: “We disavow all force and power that would be from us, and we verify ourselves by what is from You in grace and favor. Make us not a target for the arrows of Your decrees in Your vengeance; be merciful by Your tenderness and Your generosity — deliver us from those upon whom You have been wroth, whom You have humiliated and branded with the iron of separation”.

versets.ar: وَنَجِّنَا بِرَحْمَتِكَ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-najjinā bi-raḥmatika mina l-qawmi l-kāfirīna
versets.trad: save us by Your Mercy from this disbelieving people!”
versets.commentaire: “Save us by Your mercy” — not by our work, but by Your pure tenderness; it is this that draws us from the hands of deniers.

versets.ar: وَأَوْحَيْنَا إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰ وَأَخِيهِ أَن تَبَوَّآ لِقَوْمِكُمَا بِمِصْرَ بُيُوتًا وَاجْعَلُوا بُيُوتَكُمْ قِبْلَةً وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-awḥaynā ilā mūsā wa-akhīhi an tabawwaʾā li-qawmikumā bi-miṣra buyūtan wa-jʿalū buyūtakum qiblatan wa-aqīmū l-ṣalāta wa-bashshiri l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: We revealed to Moses and his brother: “Settle your people in houses in Egypt and make of your houses places of prayer. Perform the prayer, and give good tidings to the believers.”
