versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse an interior parable: He has smoothed for Our worship places — their souls; for Our gnoses dwellings — their hearts; for Our love sites — their spirits; for Our contemplation abodes — their secrets. Thus the souls of the worshippers are houses of service, the hearts of the gnostics homelands of reverence, the spirits of the enraptured sites of love, the secrets of the unifiers abodes of sacred awe.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰ رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ آتَيْتَ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَأَهُ زِينَةً وَأَمْوَالًا فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا رَبَّنَا لِيُضِلُّوا عَن سَبِيلِكَ ۖ رَبَّنَا اطْمِسْ عَلَىٰ أَمْوَالِهِمْ وَاشْدُدْ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ فَلَا يُؤْمِنُوا حَتَّىٰ يَرَوُا الْعَذَابَ الْأَلِيمَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla mūsā rabbanā innaka ātayta firʿawna wa-malaʾahu zīnatan wa-amwālan fī l-ḥayāti l-dunyā rabbanā li-yuḍillū ʿan sabīlika rabbanā ṭmis ʿalā amwālihim wa-shdud ʿalā qulūbihim fa-lā yuʾminū ḥattā yarawu l-ʿadhāba l-alīma
versets.trad: Moses said: “Our Lord! You have given Pharaoh and his notables adornments and wealth in the life of this world, so much that they have turned away from Your path. Our Lord! Wipe out their wealth, harden their hearts, and let them not believe until they see the painful chastisement.”
versets.commentaire: When Moses despaired of their response after his call, he invoked upon them the descent of wrath and the taste of separation. It is known that prophets have the right to infallibility; if Moses cursed them thus, it was, in truth, only by permission from God.

mots.racine: ش د د / ق ل ب

versets.ar: قَالَ قَدْ أُجِيبَت دَّعْوَتُكُمَا فَاسْتَقِيمَا وَلَا تَتَّبِعَانِّ سَبِيلَ الَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: qāla qad ujībat daʿwatukumā fa-staqīmā wa-lā tattabiʿānni sabīla lladhīna lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: God said: “Your supplication has been answered. Both of you, stand firm and do not follow the path of those who do not know.”
versets.commentaire: Rectitude in invocation is the abandonment of all haste to obtain the goal. And haste does not fall from the heart save by the advent of quietude (sakīna) in it; and this quietude does not arrive save by the beauty of contentment with all that manifests from the Unseen. Let the servant, through God, suffice himself as much as he can — then his invocation will be rare. Then if he invokes upon an indication from the Unseen, his duty is not to hasten and to remain peaceful.

versets.ar: وَجَاوَزْنَا بِبَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ الْبَحْرَ فَأَتْبَعَهُمْ فِرْعَوْنُ وَجُنُودُهُ بَغْيًا وَعَدْوًا ۖ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا أَدْرَكَهُ الْغَرَقُ قَالَ آمَنتُ أَنَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا الَّذِي آمَنَتْ بِهِ بَنُو إِسْرَائِيلَ وَأَنَا مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-jāwaznā bi-banī isrāʾīla l-baḥra fa-atbaʿahum firʿawnu wa-junūduhu baghyan wa-ʿadwan ḥattā idhā adrakahu l-gharaqu qāla āmantu annahu lā ilāha illā lladhī āmanat bihi banū isrāʾīla wa-anā mina l-muslimīna
versets.trad: We made the children of Israel cross the sea. Pharaoh and his armies pursued them with rebellion and hostility, until, on the point of being drowned, Pharaoh cried out: “I believe that there is no god save the One in whom the children of Israel believe, and I am among those submitted to Him!”
versets.commentaire: Majesty bore Pharaoh to throw himself into the sea after them; when perdition was verified, the necessity of stratagem pushed him to take refuge — this did not avail him, the time of choice having passed. When he saw the assaulting turn of the decree, he awoke from the intoxication of error — but: “after the sight of the calamity, neither humility nor sorrow avail”.

mots.racine: د ر ك / غ ر ق

versets.ar: آلْآنَ وَقَدْ عَصَيْتَ قَبْلُ وَكُنتَ مِنَ الْمُفْسِدِينَ
versets.tr: āl-āna wa-qad ʿaṣayta qablu wa-kunta mina l-mufsidīna
versets.trad: God said: “Now? Yet before this you were rebellious and among the corrupters.
versets.commentaire: After this long reprieve, your obstinacy in the bad act, your race in the arena of illusion, the exhaustion of the time of excuse — alas! You have deserved to be cast back in the face; no acceptance of your excuse, no access to what you desire.

versets.ar: فَالْيَوْمَ نُنَجِّيكَ بِبَدَنِكَ لِتَكُونَ لِمَنْ خَلْفَكَ آيَةً ۚ وَإِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنَ النَّاسِ عَنْ آيَاتِنَا لَغَافِلُونَ
versets.tr: fa-l-yawma nunajjīka bi-badanika li-takūna li-man khalfaka āyatan wa-inna kathīran mina l-nāsi ʿan āyātinā la-ghāfilūna
versets.trad: Yet today We shall save you in your body, that you may be a sign for those who come after you. Truly many people are heedless of Our signs.”
versets.commentaire: We shall proclaim your chastisement; We shall make manifest, to whoever sees clearly, your correction — that you may be for those who survive you a warning; and your regret, since your awakening, will increase in affliction. The preserved body becomes a visible verse.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ بَوَّأْنَا بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ مُبَوَّأَ صِدْقٍ وَرَزَقْنَاهُم مِّنَ الطَّيِّبَاتِ فَمَا اخْتَلَفُوا حَتَّىٰ جَاءَهُمُ الْعِلْمُ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَقْضِي بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ فِيمَا كَانُوا فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad bawwaʾnā banī isrāʾīla mubawwaʾa ṣidqin wa-razaqnāhum mina l-ṭayyibāti fa-mā khtalafū ḥattā jāʾahumu l-ʿilmu inna rabbaka yaqḍī baynahum yawma l-qiyāmati fīmā kānū fīhi yakhtalifūna
versets.trad: We established for the children of Israel a sure dwelling, and We dispensed to them good things for their sustenance. Differences arose among them only after Knowledge had come to them. Truly, on the Day of Resurrection, your Lord shall judge between them concerning that wherein they used to differ.
versets.commentaire: We have lowered for them the days, multiplied the blessings, honored the dwelling, opened all good things, perpetuated the goods — then, as they opposed ingratitude to blessing and persisted in injustice and aggression, We made them taste the evil chastisement and closed upon them the doors We had opened of honor.

mots.racine: ب و أ / ص د ق

versets.ar: فَإِن كُنتَ فِي شَكٍّ مِّمَّا أَنزَلْنَا إِلَيْكَ فَاسْأَلِ الَّذِينَ يَقْرَءُونَ الْكِتَابَ مِن قَبْلِكَ ۚ لَقَدْ جَاءَكَ الْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْمُمْتَرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-in kunta fī shakkin mimmā anzalnā ilayka fa-sʾali lladhīna yaqraʾūna l-kitāba min qablika laqad jāʾaka l-ḥaqqu min rabbika fa-lā takūnanna mina l-mumtarīna
versets.trad: If you are in doubt about what We have revealed to you, ask those who read the Scriptures sent before you,
themes: the interrogation of the People of the Book
versets.commentaire: The Prophet never doubted what was revealed to him, nor did he question any of them; this discourse is on the mode of raising, and its aim is to warn the people to remain in the rectitude of the way. And: the mark of the people of election is the sight of themselves and their states with the eye of diminishment.

versets.ar: وَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ فَتَكُونَ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lā takūnanna mina lladhīna kadhdhabū bi-āyāti llāhi fa-takūna mina l-khāsirīna
versets.trad: and never be of those who deny the signs of God, lest you be among the losers.
versets.commentaire: What is forbidden is so by the Law; the prohibition for the Prophet of denying God's signs is not that it would be ugly by reason — it is that it is forbidden by Law: thus falls the objection: “how would it be forbidden if it is far from him?”

mots.racine: ك ذ ب / أ ي ي

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ حَقَّتْ عَلَيْهِمْ كَلِمَتُ رَبِّكَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna ḥaqqat ʿalayhim kalimatu rabbika lā yuʾminūna
versets.trad: Truly, those against whom the word of your Lord has come true [concerning the guilty] will not believe,
versets.commentaire: The enemies: the Word of chastisement has been verified upon them; the friends: the Word of recompense has been verified upon them. The Word is eternal, the decrees prior, the acts in the future along the times following according to the substance of the decree. Those whose share in the partition is misfortune will not believe, despite all evidence and every miracle.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ جَاءَتْهُمْ كُلُّ آيَةٍ حَتَّىٰ يَرَوُا الْعَذَابَ الْأَلِيمَ
versets.tr: wa-law jāʾat-hum kullu āyatin ḥattā yarawu l-ʿadhāba l-alīma
versets.trad: even if every sign were to come to them, until they see the painful chastisement.
versets.commentaire: Even if every sign came to them, they will only believe upon seeing the chastisement — when it is too late.

mots.racine: ك ل ل / أ ي ي
ع ذ ب / أ ل م

versets.ar: فَلَوْلَا كَانَتْ قَرْيَةٌ آمَنَتْ فَنَفَعَهَا إِيمَانُهَا إِلَّا قَوْمَ يُونُسَ لَمَّا آمَنُوا كَشَفْنَا عَنْهُمْ عَذَابَ الْخِزْيِ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَمَتَّعْنَاهُمْ إِلَىٰ حِينٍ
versets.tr: fa-lawlā kānat qaryatun āmanat fa-nafaʿahā īmānuhā illā qawma yūnusa lammā āmanū kashafnā ʿanhum ʿadhāba l-khizyi fī l-ḥayāti l-dunyā wa-mattaʿnāhum ilā ḥīnin
versets.trad: Otherwise, a believing city would have been saved by its faith. But this only happened to the people of Jonah: when they believed, We removed from them the chastisement of disgrace in this world and granted them enjoyment for a time.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the people of Jonah were overtaken by the eternal mercy in the success of imploration that was granted to them; He removed from them the chastisement and turned away from them what was hanging over them of the sanction, after what they had seen of its open doors. By His mercy, they reached their supplication — not by their supplication did they reach His mercy. Such is the absolute primacy of grace over work.

mots.racine: ق و م / ي و ن س
mots.def: sole city whose faith was accepted at the eleventh hour.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / خ ز ي

versets.ar: وَلَوْ شَاءَ رَبُّكَ لَآمَنَ مَن فِي الْأَرْضِ كُلُّهُمْ جَمِيعًا ۚ أَفَأَنتَ تُكْرِهُ النَّاسَ حَتَّىٰ يَكُونُوا مُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-law shāʾa rabbuka la-āmana man fī l-arḍi kulluhum jamīʿan a-fa-anta tukrihu l-nāsa ḥattā yakūnū muʾminīna
versets.trad: If your Lord had willed, all who are on earth would have believed together. Will you then compel people to be believers?
versets.commentaire: How could a willed one resist Him — when what remains outside His will is either forgotten or vanquished? And the One who merits the majesty of power misses no sought thing. Faith is not forced; it is received.

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ لِنَفْسٍ أَن تُؤْمِنَ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۚ وَيَجْعَلُ الرِّجْسَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna li-nafsin an tuʾmina illā bi-idhni llāhi wa-yajʿalu l-rijsa ʿalā lladhīna lā yaʿqilūna
versets.trad: No soul can believe save by the permission of God. He casts disgrace upon those who do not use reason.
versets.commentaire: It is impossible to take the “permission” (idhn) in this verse except in the sense of “will” (mashīʾa), for He has commanded faith to all, and the one to whom a thing is commanded cannot be said to be unauthorized to it. But neither can one understand that no one believes save by force — that would contradict choice: so He meant: save that God will that he believe of himself, willingly. And what He has willed is, what He has not willed is not — such is the path of the People of the Sunna.

versets.ar: قُلِ انظُرُوا مَاذَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۚ وَمَا تُغْنِي الْآيَاتُ وَالنُّذُرُ عَن قَوْمٍ لَّا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: quli nẓurū mādhā fī l-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-mā tughnī l-āyātu wa-l-nudhuru ʿan qawmin lā yuʾminūna
versets.trad: Say: “Consider what is in the heavens and on the earth: neither signs nor warnings avail a people who do not believe.”
versets.commentaire: The proofs, however manifest they may be, are of no use if the inner sights are blocked — just as the suns, however risen they may be, are of no use if the eyes are closed to perception by blindness. “What profit is there for the child of this world in his pupil, if light and darkness are equivalent in him?”

versets.ar: فَهَلْ يَنتَظِرُونَ إِلَّا مِثْلَ أَيَّامِ الَّذِينَ خَلَوْا مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ قُلْ فَانتَظِرُوا إِنِّي مَعَكُم مِّنَ الْمُنتَظِرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-hal yantaẓirūna illā mithla ayyāmi lladhīna khalaw min qablihim qul fa-ntaẓirū innī maʿakum mina l-muntaẓirīna
versets.trad: What then do they await save days like those of the generations that passed before them? Say: “Wait! I too shall wait with you!”
versets.commentaire: To desire the attentions of the lights of Reality is to go out into seduction, to lean without obtaining, to sink into error — for whoever remains in blindness.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ نُنَجِّي رُسُلَنَا وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ حَقًّا عَلَيْنَا نُنجِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: thumma nunajjī rusulanā wa-lladhīna āmanū kadhālika ḥaqqan ʿalaynā nunji l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: Then We shall deliver Our messengers and those who believe, for it is just that We deliver the believers.
versets.commentaire: The particles of attribution substitute one for another: here “upon Us” means “from Us.” No thing is due upon God by obligation of His divinity; the thing is obliged from God, by His veracity — not upon Him by His might. And just as it is not admissible that a prophet enter the Fire, it is not admissible that a single believer remain eternal therein, since He has announced that He would deliver messengers and believers together.

mots.racine: ن ج و / ر س ل

versets.ar: قُلْ يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِن كُنتُمْ فِي شَكٍّ مِّن دِينِي فَلَا أَعْبُدُ الَّذِينَ تَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ وَلَٰكِنْ أَعْبُدُ اللَّهَ الَّذِي يَتَوَفَّاكُمْ ۖ وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: qul yā ayyuhā l-nāsu in kuntum fī shakkin min dīnī fa-lā aʿbudu lladhīna taʿbudūna min dūni llāhi wa-lākin aʿbudu llāha lladhī yatawaffākum wa-umirtu an akūna mina l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: Say: “O people! If you are in doubt about my religion, know that I do not worship those whom you worship besides God, but I worship God, who shall call you back to Him. I have been commanded to be among the believers.”
versets.commentaire: If you are in the veil of doubt, I am in the clarity of the Unseen; if you are in the darkness of ignorance, I am in the suns of union; if you are in the obscurity of error, I am clothed in the cloak of prophecy and the light of indications. Our paths have parted at the crossroads: you have fallen into the pit of the crooked, and I hold firm on the straightness of the path.

mots.sens: who calls you back to Him

versets.ar: وَأَنْ أَقِمْ وَجْهَكَ لِلدِّينِ حَنِيفًا وَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: wa-an aqim wajhaka li-l-dīni ḥanīfan wa-lā takūnanna mina l-mushrikīna
versets.trad: Likewise it has been said to me: “Dedicate your purpose to the Religion as a pure believer (ḥanīf). Be not among the associators.
versets.commentaire: Purify your heart for religion, strip it of the affirmation of all that the coercion of becoming has seized, lean away from deviation and innovation, enter the number of those who have pure intention in reality.

versets.ar: وَلَا تَدْعُ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ مَا لَا يَنفَعُكَ وَلَا يَضُرُّكَ ۖ فَإِن فَعَلْتَ فَإِنَّكَ إِذًا مِّنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lā tadʿu min dūni llāhi mā lā yanfaʿuka wa-lā yaḍurruka fa-in faʿalta fa-innaka idhan mina l-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: Do not invoke, besides God, what cannot profit you or harm you. If you did so, you would be among the unjust.
themes: the uselessness of invoking other than God
versets.commentaire: Do not worship that whose worship neither profits nor harms — such is the description of all that is worshipped besides God. And the recourse of creatures to creatures is destruction of time without profit; whoever does not possess for himself harm or profit, how would the one in his very state have recourse to him? If the weak adds to the weak, weakness increases.

versets.ar: وَإِن يَمْسَسْكَ اللَّهُ بِضُرٍّ فَلَا كَاشِفَ لَهُ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ وَإِن يُرِدْكَ بِخَيْرٍ فَلَا رَادَّ لِفَضْلِهِ ۚ يُصِيبُ بِهِ مَن يَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ ۚ وَهُوَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-in yamsaska llāhu bi-ḍurrin fa-lā kāshifa lahu illā huwa wa-in yuridka bi-khayrin fa-lā rādda li-faḍlihi yuṣību bihi man yashāʾu min ʿibādihi wa-huwa l-ghafūru l-raḥīmu
versets.trad: If God afflicts you with harm, none can lift it save Him. If He wills for you a good, none can turn back His grace. He bestows it on whomever He wills among His servants. He is the Forgiving, the Merciful.”
versets.commentaire: As He has singled Himself out by the production of harm and its invention, without associate to support Him, so He has unified Himself by the lifting of harm and its turning away, without helper to second Him. And Qushayrī notes with delicacy: He has lightened the harshness of harm for the believer by saying “if God touches you with a harm,” attributing it to Himself — and the colocynth is savored from the palm of the one you love. Subtlety of language: “if God touches you with a harm” — not “if He wills for you a harm” — although that harm proceeds from His will.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م

versets.ar: قُلْ يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ قَدْ جَاءَكُمُ الْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ فَمَنِ اهْتَدَىٰ فَإِنَّمَا يَهْتَدِي لِنَفْسِهِ ۖ وَمَن ضَلَّ فَإِنَّمَا يَضِلُّ عَلَيْهَا ۖ وَمَا أَنَا عَلَيْكُم بِوَكِيلٍ
versets.tr: qul yā ayyuhā l-nāsu qad jāʾakumu l-ḥaqqu min rabbikum fa-mani htadā fa-innamā yahtadī li-nafsihi wa-man ḍalla fa-innamā yaḍillu ʿalayhā wa-mā anā ʿalaykum bi-wakīlin
versets.trad: Say: “O people! The Truth has come to you from your Lord. Whoever is rightly guided is guided only for himself. And whoever strays does so only to his own detriment. I am not a guardian over you.”
versets.commentaire: Whoever becomes clear-sighted gains the profit of his own self; whoever errs has deviated from his own goal — this is an acquired trial, that is a light and healing drawn near.

versets.ar: وَاتَّبِعْ مَا يُوحَىٰ إِلَيْكَ وَاصْبِرْ حَتَّىٰ يَحْكُمَ اللَّهُ ۖ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ الْحَاكِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-ttabiʿ mā yūḥā ilayka wa-ṣbir ḥattā yaḥkuma llāhu wa-huwa khayru l-ḥākimīna
versets.trad: Follow then what is revealed to you, and have patience until God comes to judge. He is the best of judges.
versets.commentaire: The verse seals the sūra: follow the revelation received and be patient until God's judgement — He, the best of judges. This is the pure Muhammadan posture: follow, endure, entrust.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / و ح ي
mots.sens: follow what is revealed to you

mots.racine: خ ي ر / ح ك م
